found_by: Phase A rerun seal — AU-9 evidence-chain gate (built 2 hours earlier the same day)
the_finding: TWO schedulers were writing the SAME lease/dispatch ledgers the entire time. (1) the Phase A soak_runner (PID 67046, commit 28cb05bd, CHAINED writes). (2) a PersistentScheduler (PID 17807) kept alive by the launchd agent com.hoch.helm.operations (PID 12844), started 09:19, running PRE-FIX code, writing UNCHAINED rows for the SAME SOAK-* task ids. They interleaved. The package ledger chain broke at record 7.
why_this_is_the_answer_to_a_year: This is why no baseline has EVER stuck. A second scheduler nobody accounted for has been co-writing the evidence. Every 'clean' run was silently contaminated by a foreign writer. No prior instrument could detect it -- a ledger that cannot detect a second writer looks identical to one with a single writer. The AU-9 tamper-evident chain, built the same day, caught it on its FIRST real seal. Without the chain this would have sealed as a clean PASS over corrupted dual-writer evidence.
how_confirmed: package task_lease_ledger.jsonl: CHAIN BROKEN at record 7
live daemon ledger: last rows SOAK-HCF-135/SOAK-HSF-135 written UNCHAINED (prev_hash absent)
PID 17807 parent = PID 12844 = com.hoch.helm.operations (KeepAlive launchd agent)
independent parallel agent (launchd classification) flagged the SAME process as the scheduler parent
remediation_taken: launchctl bootout com.hoch.helm.operations -> supervisor 12844 GONE, phantom scheduler 17807 GONE, no respawn after 15s. Plist PRESERVED (not deleted). Restore command recorded in coordination/ops/RESTORE_helm_operations.md. Founder chose: kill now, do NOT rerun yet.
phase_a_verdict: SOAK_PHASE_A_FAIL — CONTRADICTED on AU-9 chain (split-brain co-writer). CORRECT failure.
next_when_founder_says_go: The live daemon ledger is now chain-contaminated by the phantom's unchained rows and must be reset to a clean chained baseline before a rerun. Then relaunch Phase A on the SINGLE writer.
residual_risk: com.hoch.helm-autoloop (still loaded) can spawn a COMPETING uvicorn on 0.0.0.0:8770 if its health curl fails. Not a ledger writer, but a second API. Flagged for the same treatment before rerun.
